name: Serratella and Culla Summits Loop
name_original: Ascenso al puerto de la Serratella — Circular desde Alcalà de Xivert
description: This difficult 44.3-mile road cycling route from Alcalà de Xivert features a challenging ascent to Puerto de la Serratella.
region_intro: Road cycling around Alcalà De Xivert offers a diverse landscape, blending coastal beauty with challenging inland terrain. The region is characterized by the mountainous relief of the Sierra de Irta Natural Park, featuring Mediterranean forests and scrubland. Cyclists can navigate low-traffic secondary asphalted roads that wind through historical sites and natural reserves. This area provides varied terrain, from coastal paths to routes with significant elevation gain, catering to different preferences. Best road cycling routes around Alcalà De Xivert The most popular road cycling route is The Baix Maestrat — Circular from Alcossebre, a 43.8 miles (70.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 43 minutes to complete. This difficult route features over 3,600 feet (1,110 m) of elevation gain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Serratella and Culla Summits Loop, a difficult 44.3 miles (71.2 km) path. This route includes a challenging ascent to Puerto de la Serratella, offering over 4,300 feet (1,310 m) of climbing. Local road cyclists also love the Hermitage of Saint Anthony – Torre la Sal Coastal Promenade loop from Antiga estació d'Alcossebre, a 39.4 miles (63.4 km) trail leading through coastal areas and past historical sites, often completed in about 2 hours 42 minutes. Road cycling around Alcalà De Xivert is defined by the Sierra de Irta Natural Park, Mediterranean forests, and coastal paths, often utilizing low-traffic secondary asphalted roads. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate coastal rides to difficult mountain ascents. The routes in Alcalà De Xivert are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 2,700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcalà De Xivert's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Alcalà De Xivert?
A: Road cycling around Alcalà De Xivert offers a diverse landscape, blending coastal beauty with challenging inland terrain. You'll find routes that navigate low-traffic secondary asphalted roads through the mountainous relief of the Sierra de Irta Natural Park, featuring Mediterranean forests and scrubland. This means you can experience everything from coastal paths with minimal elevation to routes with significant climbs.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Alcalà De Xivert?
A: There are over 50 road cycling routes available around Alcalà De Xivert. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 11 easy, 25 moderate, and 20 difficult options to explore.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain?
A: Yes, Alcalà De Xivert offers several challenging routes with substantial elevation gain. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e881965581/serratella-and-culla-summits-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serratella and Culla Summits Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 71.2 km path that includes a challenging ascent to Puerto de la Serratella, offering over 1,300 meters of climbing. Another demanding option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e883062711/the-baix-maestrat-circular-from-alcossebre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Baix Maestrat — Circular from Alcossebre&lt;/a&gt;, a 70.5 km route with over 1,100 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning views and pass by historical sites. You can cycle past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/244763" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Xivert Castle&lt;/a&gt;, an Islamic fortification with panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://ca.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ermita_de_Sant_Benet_i_Santa_Ll%C3%BAcia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Santa Llúcia i Sant Benet&lt;/a&gt;, located 312 meters above sea level, is another magnificent viewpoint offering extensive vistas of the coastline, including the Columbretes Islands, and even the Penyagolosa peak inland.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in the area?
A: While many routes in Alcalà De Xivert feature significant climbs, there are easier options available. With 11 routes classified as easy, families can find suitable paths, particularly those that stick to the less elevated coastal areas or flatter sections of the region. Always check the route's elevation profile and distance to ensure it matches your family's fitness level.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Alcalà De Xivert?
A: The road cycling routes in Alcalà De Xivert are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the blend of coastal beauty and challenging inland climbs, and the opportunity to explore historical sites and natural reserves on low-traffic asphalted roads.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Alcalà De Xivert are designed as circular loops. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e883062711/the-baix-maestrat-circular-from-alcossebre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Baix Maestrat — Circular from Alcossebre&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19438856" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Saint Anthony – Torre la Sal Coastal Promenade loop from Antiga estació d'Alcossebre&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate ride through coastal areas.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while road cycling?
A: The region is defined by the stunning Sierra de Irta Natural Park, where you'll encounter Mediterranean forests, scrubland, and unspoiled coastlines with steep cliffs and hidden coves. You might also pass by natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1657526" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Prat de Cabanes-Torreblanca Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; and various lakes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3106228" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bassa Llona&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5654440" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ullal de l'Estany&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking in Alcalà De Xivert?
A: Alcalà De Xivert is an excellent destination for outdoor activities, including road cycling, for much of the year. The Mediterranean climate generally offers pleasant conditions. Spring and autumn are typically ideal, providing comfortable temperatures for longer rides. Summer can be warm, especially inland, so early morning rides are recommended during those months.

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty road cycling routes that offer a good balance of challenge and scenery?
A: Absolutely. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17322560" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fortified Hermitage of Albalat – Cabanes Pass loop from Antiga estació d'Alcossebre&lt;/a&gt;, a 84.3 km route with around 795 meters of elevation gain, provide a good moderate challenge. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1672493" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Lake – Torrenostra Beach loop from Antiga estació d'Alcossebre&lt;/a&gt;, a 67 km route with similar elevation, offering a balanced experience.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Alcalà De Xivert?
A: Many routes start from towns like Alcossebre or Alcalà de Xivert itself, where public parking is generally available. For routes venturing into the Sierra de Irta Natural Park, look for designated parking areas near trailheads or points of interest. It's always advisable to check specific route details on komoot for recommended starting points and parking information.

Q: Are there any coastal road cycling routes?
A: Yes, Alcalà De Xivert's location between mountains and the Mediterranean Sea means you can enjoy beautiful coastal road cycling routes. These paths often feature crystal-clear waters and picturesque views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19438856" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Saint Anthony – Torre la Sal Coastal Promenade loop from Antiga estació d'Alcossebre&lt;/a&gt; is a great example of a route that leads through coastal areas.
tips: With its almost 14 km in length and saving just over 540 m, this 3rd category port is demanding but at the same time comforting due to the views that you will have throughout the journey, especially once up there. Go easy and enjoy it!

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route in Alcalà de Xivert?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route starting and ending in Alcalà de Xivert, you can typically find parking within the town itself. Look for public parking areas suitable for leaving your vehicle while you complete the ride.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Ascent to the port of la Serratella route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily a hard road ride with mostly well-paved surfaces. However, be prepared for some segments that may be unpaved and difficult to ride. Closer to the summit of Puerto de la Serratella, the road condition can degrade to rough concrete asphalt, which might affect descending.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as 'difficult' and a 'Hard road ride' requiring 'Very good fitness'. It involves significant elevation gain and demanding climbs, making it best suited for experienced cyclists.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Castellón province, where this route is located, is known for its pleasant climate. Generally, spring and autumn offer the most comfortable temperatures for cycling. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring colder conditions, especially at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers breathtaking scenery with open landscapes, mountains, and historic towns. The ascent to Puerto de la Serratella itself is a key highlight and provides expansive views.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Given that this is a challenging racebike route with demanding climbs and potentially rough road surfaces, it is generally not suitable for cycling with dogs. It's best enjoyed as a dedicated road cycling experience.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no special permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It uses public roads and trails.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route covers sections of several other paths, including a significant portion of the [CIMA CS06] Serratella (25%) and the Via Augusta de la Comunitat Valenciana (18%). You'll also briefly encounter parts of the Camí dels Romans and the Camí de la Fonteta dels Horts.

faqs.question: What are the key challenges of the climb to Puerto de la Serratella?
faqs.answer: The climb to Puerto de la Serratella is 15.2 kilometers long with a 603-meter vertical ascent and an average gradient of 4%. While the initial part is relatively easy, you'll encounter sections with inclines up to 10%. The road surface can also become rougher, with 'rough concrete asphalt' closer to the top.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants along the route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Alcalà de Xivert, where you will find various cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. However, due to the rural nature of the climb to Puerto de la Serratella, it's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, as facilities may be scarce on the ascent itself.

faqs.question: What kind of bike is recommended for this route?
faqs.answer: This route is specifically designed for 'racebike' cycling. Given the demanding climbs and varied road surfaces, a well-maintained road bike with appropriate gearing for ascents is highly recommended.
